findings.title: www: Executable stack (GNU_STACK rwx)
findings.description: Binary www has executable stack segment — stack buffer overflow = direct shellcode execution

findings.title: www: 4 unsafe function imports
findings.description: Binary imports 4 known-unsafe C functions: memmove, getsockname, sprintf, getsockopt. With no NX, no canary, no PIE — any overflow via these is directly exploitable.

findings.title: www: 1 functions with multiple unsafe calls

findings.title: libucrypto.so: Executable stack (GNU_STACK rwx)

findings.title: libucrypto.so: 4 unsafe function imports
findings.description: Binary imports 4 known-unsafe C functions: memcpy, getsockopt, sscanf. With no NX, no canary, no PIE — any overflow via these is directly exploitable.

findings.title: libumsg.so: Executable stack (GNU_STACK rwx)
findings.description: Binary libumsg.so has executable stack segment — stack buffer overflow = direct shellcode execution

findings.title: libumsg.so: 16 unsafe function imports
findings.description: Binary imports 16 known-unsafe C functions: execve, getsockopt, regexec, sscanf, fscanf, pthread_attr_getstack, sprintf, realpath, strncpy, fgets, memmove, getsockname, strcpy. With no NX, no canary, no PIE — any overflow via these is directly exploitable.
